elections.note: Certified final 724,596 ballots cast (SoS 'Total Voters'; 82.79% of 875,176 reg) is solid. Pre-adoption (e-pollbooks/Voter's Choice Act vote centers first used in 2020). The ROV's own Post-Election Report Exec Summary documents the election-night reporting curve: 'The first point on the upper graph - showing the number of ballots tallied as of 8 p.m. on Election Day - represents the VBM ballots that were tallied in the days prior to Election Day,' but the exact 8 p.m. first-tranche COUNT appears only in a chart image (not in the text layer), so it is not numerically sourceable. The report DOES state the FINAL Election Night report (cumulative, posted ~10:30 a.m. on 11/9 with all polling-place ballots) = 443,269 ballots = 61% of total cast; the 8 p.m. first tranche was smaller than that. Wayback (which would hold the overwritten 8 p.m. live-results capture) is blocked in this environment.

Certified final 625,425 ballots cast (SoS 'Total Voters'; 70.61% of 885,764 reg) is solid. Pre-adoption (still polling-place model; ~80% of ballots were VBM and most were processed during the multi-day canvass, not on election night). The ROV's Nov 6 2018 Post-Election Report is demographics-focused and does not publish a first-8 p.m.-report or final-election-night ballot COUNT in its text (the ballots-tallied-over-time data is shown only as a chart). No 8 p.m. first-tranche figure is sourceable here; Wayback is blocked.

PROXY, not an exact 8 p.m. first-report count. Certified final 863,964 ballots cast (SoS 'Total Voters'; 84.76% of 1,019,309 reg) is solid. First year of e-pollbooks / Voter's Choice Act vote centers (post), under COVID universal vote-by-mail. ABC7 reported ~617,000 VBM ballots had been 'received and counted' as of Monday morning Nov 2, with 631,000 received before Election Day; because a VCA county tabulates received VBM before 8 p.m. (results embargoed until polls close), the 8 p.m. first report consisted of these pre-tallied VBM ballots and was >= ~617,000. 617,000/863,964 = 71.4% is therefore a lower-bound estimate of the election-night share, reflecting the pandemic mail surge. The precise 8 p.m. live-results capture is on the overwritten Clarity ENR page (Wayback blocked). News also reported the county planned to release 450,000-500,000 ballots at 8 p.m. (San Jose Inside).

Certified final 550,602 ballots cast (SoS 'Total Voters'; 54.55% of 1,009,422 reg) is solid (the ROV's own Post-Election Dashboard reports a near-identical 551,267). Post-adoption (VCA / e-pollbooks). Per the ROV, the first results posted shortly after 8 p.m. are the early-returned vote-by-mail ballots, followed by vote-center results later that night. The exact 8 p.m. first-tranche count lives only in the ROV's interactive Post-Election Dashboard 'vote tallying' chart (rendered inside an iframe; not extractable as a number here) and the overwritten Clarity ENR live page (Wayback blocked). No 8 p.m. ballot count is numerically sourceable in this environment.

Certified final 765,495 ballots cast (SoS 'Total Voters'; 72.99% of 1,048,723 reg) is solid (certified 12/5/2024). Post-adoption (VCA / e-pollbooks). The first 8 p.m. report was the early-returned VBM ballots (San Jose Spotlight noted ~480,000 mail ballots, ~43%, had been returned heading into Election Day - a returns figure, not a count). The exact 8 p.m. first-tranche ballot count is only in the ROV's iframe-walled Post-Election Dashboard chart and the overwritten Clarity ENR live page (results.enr.clarityelections.com/CA/Santa_Clara/122582/web.345435); Wayback is blocked, so it is not numerically sourceable here.
